data.title: Palantir CEO speaks out against 'tokenmaxxing' being pushed by frontier labs
data.description:
Palantir (PLTR) CEO Alex Karp spoke out against the token pricing model being pushed by frontier labs such as Anthropic (ANTHRO) and OpenAI (OPENAI) during an interview with CNBC on Wednesday.

"I'm not throwing shade at them, but something has gone completely wrong," he told CNBC. "The basic view among enterprises in this country is I'm going to chillax and waste my time with tokens."

Palantir closed with a nearly 8% gain on Wednesday.

The subsidized AI ride appears to be over as some enterprises are finding out the hard way about the rising costs of new models and surging token usage. This is prompting some businesses to lean into open-weight models that are typically cheaper to use.

"Tokenmaxxing hijacks your value orientation and decreases your institutional fortitude and intelligence," Palantir said on X in a nine-point post on the importance of AI sovereignty. "The pursuit of high token usage incentivizes disposable scripts over robust software—with the addictive feeling of false progress. There is a reason why those selling tokens refuse to charge based on value."

Some businesses are also developing their own AI models that are customized to meet the specific needs of the enterprise or agency. Earlier this week, Palantir revealed a new collaboration with Nvidia (NVDA). It uses Nvidia's open-source Nemotron models to help the government develop custom AI systems while reducing the security and proprietary issues associated with closed models.

"Your AI sovereignty dictates your institution's future," Palantir said on X. "Sovereignty is the precondition for choice. Relinquishing sovereignty transfers the future choices of your institution to others, who are likely to exploit it for their gain and your loss."

"What aligns me with Nvidia, and I think is what the technical customers want, which is control over their compute, their models, their data stack and their alpha," Karp said to CNBC. "They want to know they own the means of production. It’s not being transferred to someone else."

data.title: Global-E Online closes on Passport acquisition as it adds to its shipping capabilities
data.description:
Global-E Online Ltd. (GLBE) closed on its $350M acquisition of Passport Global on Wednesday. The deal to pick up the U.S.-based cross-border e-commerce logistics and solutions company is expected to further strengthen Global-E's (GLBE) logistics capabilities, adding advanced standard shipping solutions powered by an efficient, asset-light, multi-carrier network across cross-border, domestic, and last-mile deliveries. 

The capabilities and network from Passport will also enhance Global-e's (GLBE) ability to execute direct injection and consolidated returns, supporting the delivery of scalable and cost-effective logistics solutions. In addition, through the Passport brand, Global-e (GLBE) will provide a non-Merchant of Record (non-MoR) solution to address and provide support for certain merchant segments.

"By bringing Passport’s advanced cross-border logistics infrastructure and asset-light, multi-carrier network together with our end-to-end platform, we can deliver more reliable and cost-effective shipping, faster delivery, and a better post-purchase experience, creating real, tangible value for our merchants and their customers around the world," highlighted Global-e (GLBE) President Nir Debbi.

The acquisition was funded via approximately equal portions of cash on hand and ordinary shares of Global-e (GLBE). An additional contingent consideration of up to $75M is subject to Passport achieving certain financial results.

Passport is expected to generate approximately $100M in revenues in calendar year 2026 with a rapid growth rate, which is expected to slightly exceed that of Global-e (GLBE) for the year. The acquisition is expected to have a neutral to slightly positive effect on Global-E's (GLBE) adjusted EBITDA contribution in the second half of 2026.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Thursday
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) declining due to technology stocks suffering ongoing volatility.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.2%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) ended -0.7%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished flat after having reached a new all-time high mid-session.

Now, here are three things to look out for on Thursday:

  • Because the U.S. markets will be closed on Friday in observance of Independence Day, the Bureau of Labor Statistics is releasing its crucial monthly jobs report a day early. The U.S. economy is expected to add 114K jobs in June, down from the 172K estimated in May, according to the average estimate of economists.
  • Releasing concurrently with the nonfarm payrolls report, jobless claims data will provide a window into the latest corporate layoff trends. Initial claims are expected to be 219K in the past week, compared with the earlier 215K print.
  • CrowdStrike's (CRWD) four-for-one stock split will become effective when the market opens.

data.title: U.S. stocks decline at the start of the second half of the year as tech struggles
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) declining due to technology stocks suffering ongoing volatility.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.2%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) ended -0.7%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished flat after having reached a new all-time high mid-session.

Among the worst decliners within tech were Micron Technology (MU) -10.1%, Sandisk (SNDK) -10.6%, Nvidia (NVDA) -1.2%, and Broadcom (AVGO) -2.2%.

Meta Platforms (META), on the other hand, closed +8.9% after Bloomberg reported it is building a cloud business for excess artificial intelligence compute.

“July 1st was a rather humdrum day for the market,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones. “It seems as though the market is largely in waiting mode as it seeks additional data for what the future might hold. And, during that time, it is de-risking some of the most expensive companies out there.”

“We are continuing to see economic conditions deteriorate. And a lot of this can be attributed to inflationary pressures that can be traced back to tariffs and the war in Iran. If I had to guess, I would say that, before the year is out, we will enter into a stagflationary recession. And that certainly would not bode well for market participants,” Jones added.

On the economic side, the ISM Manufacturing PMI fell more than expected in June, while PMI Manufacturing unexpectedly dropped.

In addition, construction spending ticked up in May, slightly lower than expected, and the U.S. private sector added 98,000 jobs in June, missing the +117,000 consensus, according to ADP.

Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that the Fed will “chart a new course” on forward guidance, contrasting with his predecessor's practice of indicating which way policymakers were leaning in upcoming meetings.

Bond yields were mostly muted. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point higher at 4.48%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 2 basis points to 4.18%.

The U.S. and Iran are conducting negotiations in Doha that focus on the Strait of Hormuz. The Trump administration is trying to convince Iran that it will gain more from a nuclear deal than from tolls in the strait.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $68, while Brent (CO1:COM) was $71 per barrel.

data.title: Warsh taps Bessent aide to serve as adviser - report
data.description:
Samantha Schwab, a senior aide to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, is set to become an adviser to Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh, according to a media report on Wednesday.

Bloomberg News reported the news, citing two people familiar with the matter.

Since April, she has served as the principal deputy chief of staff to Bessent. She joined the Treasury Department in January 2025 and served in the White House during President  Donald Trump's first term. Bloomberg said it's not clear when she'll begin her job at the central bank.

Schwab is the granddaughter of Charles Schwab, the billionaire and founder of the brokerage firm bearing his name (SCHW).

data.title: Dip buyers on overdrive: Retail investors smash records in H1
data.description:
Retail investors have demonstrated unprecedented confidence in the stock market this year, aggressively scooping up equities during market pullbacks at record levels.

According to recent analysis by Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner, retail market participants purchased nearly 3.5 times their average daily net notional amount on days when the S&P 500 (SPY) declined during the first half of 2026.

This marks the strongest "buy-the-dip" behavior ever recorded in Citadel Securities' dataset.

The chart below, tracking yearly data since 2020, illustrates a massive spike in retail capital deployment on down days this year. The 2026 buying volume dramatically dwarfs previous years, easily surpassing the previous retail frenzy seen in 2021.

Furthermore, this bullish sentiment has not been limited exclusively to market declines. The data reveals that even on days when the S&P 500 (SPY) rallied, retail investors continued to accumulate shares, buying at nearly 1.5 times the daily average.

data.title: AI to drive S&P 500 gains through 2027 - Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo Investment Institute's Scott Wren is betting on artificial intelligence (AIQ) to fuel further substantial market gains through next year, projecting nearly 25% earnings growth for the S&P 500 (SPY) in 2026 followed by a 13% gain in 2027.

The bullish outlook comes despite recent weakness in the market's most heavily weighted sectors, including information Technology (XLK) and communication services (XLC)—which together account for nearly 47% of the S&P 500's (SPY) total market capitalization. Still, Wells Fargo remains overweight on the tech sector, though Wren advises investors to "buy on pullbacks and not chase the market higher."

Beyond the mega-cap tech names, Wren sees compelling AI-related opportunities emerging across sectors that fly under the radar. Industrials (XLI), utilities (XLU), and materials (XLB) all stand to benefit from artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, while financials (XLF)—Wells Fargo's most highly rated sector—are positioned to capitalize on an accelerated volume of initial public offerings and mergers and acquisitions activity, a substantial portion of which will be tied to AI. Though financials have lagged year-to-date, they've shown improved performance over the past month.

Evidence of broadening market participation supports Wren's constructive stance. Last week, both the S&P 500 Equal Weight Index (RSP) and the Russell 2000 Index (IWM) hit record highs, as did the health care (XLV), industrials (XLI), financials (XLF), and utilities (XLU) sectors. The percentage of S&P 500 (SPY) companies trading above their 200-day moving averages has climbed steadily to 63%, signaling that the underlying health of the market may be stronger than tech-focused headlines suggest.

data.title: Alibaba, U.S. payments unit to pay $600M to settle U.S. probe
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) and U.S.-based payment processor AUS Merchant Services have agreed to pay a combined $600 million under non-prosecution agreements to resolve U.S. allegations that they failed to prevent the sale and import of illegal pharmaceuticals and related products into the United States, the U.S. Department of Justice said on Wednesday.

The Justice Department said Alibaba (BABA) admitted that merchants on its Alibaba.com and AliExpress platforms conducted about 80,000 illegal product sales into the United States between 2016 and 2024, with a combined gross merchandise value exceeding $200 million.

AUS admitted deficiencies in its anti-money laundering and transaction monitoring systems that allowed some merchants to use its payment services to facilitate the sales.

Under the agreements, Alibaba will pay a $125 million criminal penalty and forfeit $200 million, while AUS will pay an $85 million criminal penalty and forfeit $190 million. Both companies also agreed to strengthen their compliance programs.

data.title: Warsh chooses ex-BOE chief for new Fed communications task force - report
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh picked Mervyn King, a former Bank of England governor, to co-chair the U.S. central bank's new task force on communications, according to a media report on Wednesday.

King's office confirmed the appointment, Bloomberg News reported.

The move is the next step for the new Fed chair to make good on his promise for “regime change” to ensure that the Fed sticks to its congressional mandate of maximum employment and price stability. During his Senate confirmation hearing, he emphasized that the Fed should "stay in its lane," having previously accused it of straying from its mission.

King led the Bank of England from 2003 until 2013, steering the central bank through the 2008-'09 crisis. During his time at the BOE, he brought more transparency to the bank's communications policy, dumping the prior "mystery and mystique" approach.  He also created the so-called Inflation Report, a quarterly analysis of the UK's economic outlook that's now known as the Monetary Policy Report, to guide investors on policy.

About a month after Warsh became Fed chair in May, he announced the formation of five task forces to thoroughly review a wide range of the central bank's operations and policies, including its communications policies. On Wednesday, he said he'd likely announce the outside experts in the groups next week.

Warsh has already made known his dislike of forward guidance and has deflected questions from journalists about how the Federal Open Market Committee is thinking about the interest rate path. Part of the communications task force review will focus on the FOMC's Summary of Economic Projections, which includes the closely watched dot plot illustrating FOMC members' projections on future rate decisions. At an ECB conference on Wednesday, Warsh said, "There will be dots for a short time, at the very least, but we have a task force for that."

data.title: Steel Partners comes out against `value-destructive' CEO-led buyout offer for InMode
data.description:
Steel Partners, whose $18 offer to buy InMode was rejected in February, said it has “serious concerns” about a $16.20 CEO-led buyout offer.

"Let us be direct," Steel Partners Executive Chairman Warren G. Lichtenstein  wrote in a letter to InMode's board on Tuesday. "What we have seen at InMode over the last six months is a series of disastrous governance failures that have created a situation wherein its own CEO may be able to acquire the Company at a price below what the Board previously rejected. Allowing this to happen would be a travesty for shareholders."

Steel Partners is calling on the board to retain a “truly independent” investment bank, according to the letter. The investor also wants the board to form a “genuinely independent” special committee of directors. Steel is also pushing for a sales process free of CEO Moshe Mizrahy's "interference."  The firm also wants Mizrahy to step down as CEO for the duration of the process and for InMode to halt all share purchases.

"This Board still has the opportunity to do its job," Lichtenstein added. "We urge you to take it and to act in the interests of all shareholders—not those of the Chief Executive Officer to whom many of you appear beholden."

InMode didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

The Steel letter comes after fellow InMode (INMD) shareholder DOMA Perpetual Capital Management on Friday said that it wouldn't support the $16.20 proposal and would vote against it.
